<findings>

[suggestion] [info] Rename zone from 'viewer-call-rate-limiter' to 'messaging-rate-limiter': the 'viewer-' prefix implies viewer-client-only scope but web-integration (server-adjacent) also imports this zone, making the name architecturally misleading to contributors.
[suggestion] [info] Zone name carries 'viewer-' prefix but source file is 'call-rate-limiter.ts' (no prefix). Adopt a consistent naming rule: either all zone members use the prefix in filenames or the zone name drops the prefix — the current mismatch makes file-to-zone mapping non-obvious.

</findings>

<insights>

- The integration test file (request-dedup.test.ts) lives here rather than in the message zone, suggesting request deduplication is considered part of the rate-limiting contract rather than the coalescing/throttling layer — confirm this split is intentional
- The .gitkeep in tests/integration/ implies integration test coverage is planned but incomplete; the lone request-dedup test may be the seed of a larger suite
- This zone is an entry point depended on by web-viewer (1 import), making it a public infrastructure boundary — its API surface should be kept minimal and stable
- Cohesion (0.67) and coupling (0.33) are healthy, indicating a well-scoped utility boundary with limited external dependencies.
- The integration .gitkeep suggests test scaffolding was added speculatively; either populate it with meaningful integration tests or remove the placeholder to reduce noise.
- Imports from both the message zone and the web scaffolding zone (web-integration → message: 2, web-integration → web: 1) indicate this zone depends on both lower-level messaging primitives and package-level infrastructure — verify the web dependency is not pulling in heavier server-side concerns.
- web-viewer imports this zone directly (1 import) while also importing viewer-message-flow-control directly (4 imports) — the consumer bypasses the rate-limiter's composition of messaging primitives and imports both layers independently, which risks web-viewer reimplementing throttling logic already encapsulated here
- web-viewer holds direct imports into both viewer-call-rate-limiter and viewer-message-flow-control simultaneously; if viewer-call-rate-limiter is meant to be the composed interface over raw primitives, web-viewer should import only through the rate-limiter, not both layers
- The .gitkeep in tests/integration/ is counted as a zone member, inflating the file count to 4 and skewing cohesion/coupling metrics; true functional file count is 3, making the 0.67 cohesion score an overestimate of the zone's actual health
- request-dedup.test.ts is the only integration test in the zone and tests a concern (deduplication) that is semantically distinct from rate-limiting — its presence here without a corresponding source file for deduplication logic suggests either a missing source file or a misplaced test
- packages/web/tests/integration/request-dedup.test.ts tests request deduplication behavior but lives in the rate-limiter zone, which has no deduplication source file. Either a deduplication source file is missing from this zone or the test belongs in viewer-message-flow-control where coalescing primitives reside. The mismatch means the zone's stated responsibility (rate limiting) and its test coverage (deduplication) are misaligned.
- packages/web/tests/integration/.gitkeep is a non-functional placeholder that inflates zone file count, distorts cohesion/coupling metrics, and adds noise to the zone boundary. It should be removed unless a concrete integration test is planned and tracked.
- The zone name uses a 'viewer-' prefix signaling viewer-client-side scope, but web-integration (a server-adjacent zone) also imports this zone — the 'viewer-' prefix is architecturally misleading and should be renamed to 'call-rate-limiter' or 'messaging-rate-limiter' to reflect its actual shared-infrastructure role
- The source file 'call-rate-limiter.ts' omits the 'viewer-' prefix that the zone name carries — there is a systematic prefix inconsistency between zone naming and file naming across all messaging zones that will cause confusion when navigating by zone name vs file name
- Rename zone from 'viewer-call-rate-limiter' to 'messaging-rate-limiter': the 'viewer-' prefix implies viewer-client-only scope but web-integration (server-adjacent) also imports this zone, making the name architecturally misleading to contributors.
- Zone name carries 'viewer-' prefix but source file is 'call-rate-limiter.ts' (no prefix). Adopt a consistent naming rule: either all zone members use the prefix in filenames or the zone name drops the prefix — the current mismatch makes file-to-zone mapping non-obvious.
- [call graph] 104 internal calls, 9 outgoing, 2 incoming (cohesion: 0.92, coupling: 0.08)
